feedback.text: Hello, I am writing this feedback because there is an issue dear to me that isn't mentioned in the Digital Fairness Act which I believe really needs to be. It is the issue of video-game publishers rendering games unplayable after selling them to customers. Those publishers sell game with no end-of-life plan, meaning once support for the game ends, paying customers are deprived of the product they have payed for. In my opinion this practice is unacceptable and I believe the Digital Fairness Act could be a step forward in stopping this practice.
